caseQuestion: Was the launch justified despite known public-risk concerns?

turns.text: The prosecution submits that the launch proceeded with clear notice of avoidable risk. The decision was not forced; it was chosen.
The defense will show that the team acted under severe time pressure, incomplete data, and a reasonable belief that the safeguards were sufficient.
The project log records three escalation notes in the week before launch, including one warning that the failure mode had not been fully reproduced.
Ethically, the core issue is foreseeability. Once harm becomes plausible and preventable, responsibility attaches to the people who still choose to proceed.
A plausible risk is not the same as a probable outcome. Decision-makers are allowed to rely on expert mitigation when the record is still unsettled.
The record is simple: warning, delay option, conscious override. That is enough for this court to find the judgment reckless.
